destinations.evidence: The DKMS decode arm uses the DKMS request codec and returns that request executor and TTL.
The dedicated DKMS codec defines the DKMS ABI and validates integer widths for ttl, key_index, and key_format.
destinations.snippet:
let param_types = vec![
    ParamType::Address,
    ParamType::Array(Box::new(ParamType::Bytes)),
    ParamType::Uint(256),
    ParamType::Array(Box::new(ParamType::Bytes)),
    ParamType::Bytes,
    ParamType::Address,
    ParamType::Uint(256),
    ParamType::Uint(8),
];
if tokens.len() != 8 { return Err(...); }
executor: address_token(&tokens[0], "executor")?,
ttl: u64_token(&tokens[2], "ttl")?,
owner: address_token(&tokens[5], "owner")?,
key_index: u64_token(&tokens[6], "key_index")?,
key_format: u8_token(&tokens[7], "key_format")?,

equivalence_argument: For current extraction, DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE is handled by a dedicated DkmsKeyRequest branch before the generic ExecutorRequest fallback, so executor and TTL come from the DKMS request decode.
notes: The codec has since evolved to the eight-field base-plus-DKMS ABI, but the dedicated DKMS extraction path remains present.
rationale: DKMS has a different ABI from ExecutorRequest, so builder detection and validation helpers need the dedicated decoder to keep executor/TTL extraction correct. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async precompile data extraction for DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE must decode DkmsKeyRequest and return that request's executor and ttl rather than using the generic ExecutorRequest decoder.
suggested_falsification_tests: Provide a valid DKMS ABI payload whose layout would not decode as a generic ExecutorRequest and assert detector extraction and try_decode_precompile_data return the DKMS executor and ttl; corrupt the DKMS ABI and assert a DkmsKeyRequest decode error.
summary: DKMS executor and TTL extraction uses the DKMS codec.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Provide a valid DKMS ABI payload whose layout would not decode as a generic ExecutorRequest and assert detector extraction and try_decode_precompile_data return the DKMS executor and ttl; corrupt the DKMS ABI and assert a DkmsKeyRequest decode error.
title: DKMS detector extracts executor and TTL from DKMS ABI
